name: diffprivlib (IBM Differential Privacy Library)

performance.notes: NumPy-based; DP mechanisms add Laplace/Gaussian noise overhead only

agent_readiness.notes: Sklearn-compatible differential privacy library. from diffprivlib.models import GaussianNB, LinearRegression, LogisticRegression, PCA, KMeans. clf = GaussianNB(epsilon=1.0, bounds=([0, 0, 0], [1, 1, 1])). clf.fit(X_train, y_train). Drop-in sklearn replacement. Mechanisms: from diffprivlib.mechanisms import Laplace, Gaussian, ExponentialCategorical. lap = Laplace(epsilon=1, sensitivity=1). lap.randomise(value). Budget tracking: from diffprivlib.accountant import BudgetAccountant. acc = BudgetAccountant(epsilon=3, delta=1e-5). Used with models via accountant param. Tools: from diffprivlib.tools import mean, std, var. dp_mean = mean(X, epsilon=1, bounds=(0, 1)).

gotchas: bounds parameter required for most models — must provide data range
epsilon budget management manual unless BudgetAccountant used
sklearn compatibility is partial — not all sklearn params supported
No deep learning support — use Opacus for neural networks
best_when: Differentially private sklearn-compatible ML: GaussianNB, LinearRegression, LogisticRegression, PCA
DP statistics: mean, std, histogram on sensitive tabular data
Simple DP mechanisms (Laplace, Gaussian) without neural networks
avoid_when: When Opacus's PyTorch integration is needed
summary: diffprivlib provides sklearn-compatible differentially private ML. GaussianNB(epsilon=1.0, bounds=...).fit(X, y). DP statistics: mean(), std(). Mechanisms: Laplace, Gaussian, Exponential. BudgetAccountant for epsilon tracking. MIT by IBM Research. Best for DP tabular ML without deep learning. bounds parameter required. Budget must be managed manually. No deep learning (use Opacus). Sklearn-compatible drop-in for NB, LR, PCA, KMeans.
